general-qwen3-30b.description: Best default general chat/reasoning model; enable/disable thinking according to your frontend.

general-qwen3-30b-q5.description: Higher-quality general Qwen3 option; use only if your context length and runtime overhead still fit.

coding-qwen3-coder-30b.description: Primary coding, repository edits, debugging, tool use, and agentic programming.

coding-qwen3-coder-30b-long-context.description: Coding model variant intended for very large-context workflows; VRAM limits will constrain usable context.

coding-qwen3-coder-next.description: Alternative coding-agent model. Use the first shard only; llama.cpp loads the remaining shards automatically.

roleplay-qwen3-30b-abliterated.description: Creative, less-refusal-prone chat/roleplay option. Treat it as an unaligned model and keep normal safety boundaries.

roleplay-mistral-rp-24b.description: Dedicated roleplay/storytelling alternative; Q5 is a good target on 30 GB VRAM.

roleplay-mistral-small-24b.description: Higher-fidelity 24B roleplay model; use Q6 only with moderate context.

creative-hexis-24b.description: Creative/persona-heavy chat and long-form character interaction.

fast-general-qwen3-14b.description: Fast, high-quality general model with much more KV-cache headroom than 30B MoE models.

fast-coder-qwen2.5-coder-14b.description: Reliable lower-latency coding fallback, especially for medium-sized files and interactive work.

small-fast-qwen3-8b.description: Fast general assistant and utility model with generous context capacity.

small-fast-qwen2.5-coder-7b.description: Lightweight coding fallback for quick completions, scripts, and lower latency.
